31.reason: [When the stack is empty, this function prints "error " but still returns an undefined (garbage) value because there's no return statement in this branch. The calling code then prints that garbage value as "%d ", leading to incorrect output.]

47.reason: [This always tries to print the return value of pop(), even when pop() prints "error ". When the stack is empty, pop() prints "error " but returns an undefined value, which is then printed as an integer followed by a space, causing malformed output like "error <garbage> ".]

49.reason: [The loop condition is incorrect. 'scanf' returns the number of successfully read items, not the value of 'op'. When input ends, scanf returns EOF (typically -1), but 'op' will hold the last successfully read value. Thus, the loop may never terminate or behave incorrectly. Also, the problem specifies that -1 marks end of operations, but this loop doesn't check for op == -1.]
